customer_anxiety_profile: Analytical cost-skeptic who pre-loaded a $20K/$100/month ROI frame and concluded solar probably doesn't pencil out. His skepticism is math-based, not emotional or scam-driven. He moved to committed once incentives were introduced, but his enthusiasm is anchored to incentive claims Holland couldn't fully substantiate. He is not yet a homeowner — closing on the property Monday; appointment is Tuesday at 5PM MT.
unresolved_concerns: Payback period / ROI — Jim's original $20K/$100/month math was never directly addressed with real numbers. The closer needs to anchor the actual design against Jim's specific bill, which was never captured.

Incentives specifics — Holland referenced 'state incentives' and 'veteran incentives' without naming them. Jim is expecting these to materially reduce cost. If they don't apply or don't exist, the closer needs to manage that gap at the top of the call.

Tree shading on main roof — Jim acknowledged the trees and said he'd look at them after closing. The garage roof was identified as the primary panel surface. Closer should confirm tree status and lead with the garage roof design.
sensitive_topics_raised: Pre-closing status — Jim does not yet own the home as of the call. Closing is Monday. The appointment is Tuesday. If closing is delayed, the appointment may need to be rescheduled.
No utility bill captured — Jim was driving home from work during the call. No bill data was collected. Closer will need to collect bill or kWh usage at the start of the appointment.
exact_promises_rep_made: Tuesday at 5PM MT appointment with a specialist
Specialist will email over the information and walk Jim through it
Holland will text Jim appointment details after the call
Specialist will try to cover as much energy offset as possible and will show Jim the best design for his home
closer_should_know: Jim's $20K/$100/month ROI math is entirely self-constructed — he has never seen a real solar design. He is a first-look customer with no competing quote. His buy-in was built on incentives claims Holland couldn't fully name; the closer should either confirm those incentives apply to Jim or reframe early before the design presentation. The garage roof is the primary panel surface (no tree shading, good sun exposure, ~5 years old per inspector). No bill was captured — closer should collect kWh usage at the top of the call.
